message:
feat(cli): add 'What next?' success footers for spawn and quick (ntm-952.2.3)

Implement a "What's next?" suggestion system that guides users to their
next action after successful commands. This complements the error
remediation hints by providing actionable guidance for success cases too.

New success footer system in internal/output/success.go:
- Suggestion struct with Command and Description fields
- SuccessFooter() prints themed suggestions to terminals only
- Automatically skips output when piped (non-terminal detection)
- Respects NO_COLOR env var for accessibility
- Pre-built suggestion sets for common commands:
  - SpawnSuggestions: attach, dashboard, palette
  - QuickSuggestions: cd, spawn
  - AddSuggestions: status, send, dashboard
  - SendSuggestions: dashboard, attach
  - KillSuggestions: list, spawn

Commands updated:
- spawn: shows attach/dashboard/palette suggestions after success
- quick: uses new footer system for consistent "What's next?" output

Design decisions:
- Footers only appear in interactive terminals (not pipes/JSON/robot)
- Commands are shown with # comments for quick copy-paste
- Color output uses theme-aware styling (Info for commands, Overlay for descriptions)
- JSON mode gets NewSuccessWithSuggestions() for programmatic access

Test coverage:
- All suggestion builder functions
- FormatSuggestions for non-interactive formatting
- SuccessFooter and SuccessCheck buffer output
- NewSuccessWithSuggestions JSON response

Part of the Premium UX Polish epic (ntm-952.2).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -650,6 +650,9 @@ func spawnSessionLogic(opts SpawnOptions) error {
 
 	fmt.Printf("✓ Launched %d agent(s)\n", totalAgents)
 
+	// Print "What's next?" suggestions
+	output.SuccessFooter(output.SpawnSuggestions(opts.Session)...)
+
 	// Emit session_create event
 	events.EmitSessionCreate(opts.Session, opts.CCCount, opts.CodCount, opts.GmiCount, dir, opts.RecipeName)
